text.title: LLM-Powered Conversational AI Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the llm-powered-conversational-ai methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Spec MUST state per-stage latency budgets summing to <2.5 s voice or <4 s text.
rule.rationale: Above these thresholds users perceive the agent as broken.

rule.statement: Jailbreak detection + factual grounding MUST be wired between LLM output and TTS.
rule.rationale: Raw LLM output ships hallucinations + jailbreak payloads.

rule.statement: Confidence below threshold MUST trigger fallback to human or rule-based path.
rule.rationale: Hallucinated answers in production trigger trust loss.

rule.statement: Conversation history MUST be bounded by token + turn count.
rule.rationale: Unbounded history blows the model's context window.

rule.statement: Every turn MUST emit an audit log (user input + LLM output + safety verdict + latency).
rule.rationale: Without audit trail incidents are unreproducible.

rule.statement: When preconditions hold, run the full procedure end-to-end and emit the output artefact.
rule.rationale: Routes positive decision-tree leaves to the procedure.

rule.statement: When preconditions fail or skip-conditions hold, exit without producing the artefact and document why.
rule.rationale: Routes negative decision-tree leaves out without polluting the output channel.
